title: NITI Aayog
lead: Established in ==2015== to replace the Planning Commission — a paradigm shift from ==top-down planning to cooperative and competitive federalism==.

items: ==Decentralised planning== — bottom-up approach for India's diverse needs
==Cooperative federalism== — states as active partners in national strategy
==Inclusive & sustainable development== — focus on vulnerable sections
==Aspirational Districts Programme== — convergence, collaboration, competition; measurable gains in health, education, agriculture, infrastructure.
==Atal Innovation Mission== — Atal Tinkering Labs in schools, Atal Incubation Centres for startups.
==Strategy for New India @75== — roadmap across 41 sectors via stakeholder consultation.

head: Sector
Case study
Impact
rows: Rainwater harvesting for well recharge via community participation.
Village-level banking; reduced leakages, better scheme access.
Community-based palliative care with home visits.
Decentralised planning institutionalised at district level.
Real-time app monitoring of child health and nutrition.

items.title: Challenges
bullets: ==No financial powers== — unlike the Planning Commission, it allocates no funds
Coordination friction in the federal structure; ==variable state participation== (boycotts)
Perceived ==partisanship==; methodological critiques of its indices
Advisory role + weak accountability = ==limited ability to drive change==

items.title: Solutions
bullets: Institutionalised consultation with states, civil society, private sector
Capacity building at state/local levels; ==open data== and transparent monitoring; scale innovative, inclusion-focused models

blocks.text: NITI Aayog was born to give shape to India's aspirations, not just its allocations. Without financial autonomy or legislative teeth, it risks becoming a vision without velocity. To truly steer India's development story, it must evolve from a think tank to a do tank — grounded in innovation, empowered by federal trust, and driven by measurable impact.
